events.description: Senator James E. Murray introduced S. 716, a bill authorizing the issuance of a patent in fee to George Peters, read twice and referred to the Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs
George Peters wrote a letter to Senator Murray, prompting Murray to prepare and introduce S. 716
Senator Murray replied to George Peters advising him of the introduction of the new bill to authorize issuance of a patent in fee
Oscar L. Chapman, Undersecretary of the Interior, submitted report on S. 716 recommending enactment if amended to allow sale to a Crow Indian rather than issuance of patent in fee
Joseph C. O'Mahoney, Chairman of Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs, wrote to Senator Murray attaching Department of Interior report on S. 716 and asking if amendments were agreeable
House Committee on Public Lands reported S. 716 favorably without amendment, committed to Committee of the Whole House and ordered to be printed as Report No. 508
Senator Murray wrote to George Peters advising that S. 716 passed the Senate on April 11th and had been reported favorably out of the House Committee on Public Lands, enclosing copy of House report
S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
Senator Murray sent telegram to George Peters at Wyola, Montana, advising that S. 716 passed the House and was cleared for President's signature
George Peters indicated his desire to sell his land to George Redfield, another Crow Indian
Senate Committee on Interior and Insular Affairs amended S. 716 at suggestion of Department of Interior to permit Secretary to sell land under existing regulations rather than issue patent in fee

financial_transactions.description: Proposed sale of 840 acres of George Peters' land on Crow Indian Reservation to another Crow Indian, to be authorized by Secretary of the Interior under existing regulations
